title: Confusion After Fall - Chronic Subdural Hematoma
chief_complaint: 78-year-old male with progressive confusion, headache, and left-sided weakness over 2 weeks. Minor fall 3 weeks ago. On warfarin for atrial fibrillation.

physical_exam.neuro: Confused, GCS 13, left pronator drift, mild left hemiparesis (4/5), no papilledema

hidden_data.ct_head: Crescent-shaped (concave-convex) hypodense collection over right cerebral convexity, 14mm thickness, midline shift 8mm left

correct_diagnosis: Chronic Subdural Hematoma with Mass Effect (Warfarin-related)
key_learning_points: Elderly + anticoagulation + minor trauma = SDH risk

patient_responses.fall: I slipped in the bathroom 3 weeks ago, didn't hit my head hard
patient_responses.confusion: My family says I've been more forgetful
patient_responses.warfarin: I take warfarin for my heart, 5mg daily

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Chronic SDH in elderly on anticoagulation.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a chronic SDH. The crescent-shaped collection + INR confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Chronic SDH: Reverse warfarin with vitamin K + PCC. Burr hole evacuation if symptomatic or thickness >10mm with mass effect. May recur - follow-up CT. Consider subdural drain post-op.
